doc_title: A Survey of Hallucination in "Large" Foundation Models

items.fact: The paper cites 'GPT-3' by Brown et al., 2020.
The paper cites 'Stable Diffusion' by Rombach et al., 2022.
The paper 'A Stitch in Time Saves Nine: Detecting and Mitigating Hallucinations of LLMs by Validating Low-Confidence Generation (Varshney et al., 2023)' uses the WikiBio dataset.
entities: A Stitch in Time Saves Nine: Detecting and Mitigating Hallucinations of LLMs by Validating Low-Confidence Generation
items.evidence_span: A Stitch in Time Saves Nine: Detecting and Mitigating Hallucinations of LLMs by Validating Low-Confidence Generation (Varshney et al., 2023) | $\checkmark$ | $\checkmark$ | Article generation | WikiBio | Percentage of mitigated hallucinations

items.fact: The paper 'Dehallucinating Large Language Models Using Formal Methods Guided Iterative Prompting (Jha et al., 2023)' focuses on dialog tasks.
entities: Dehallucinating Large Language Models Using Formal Methods Guided Iterative Prompting
items.evidence_span: Dehallucinating Large Language Models Using Formal Methods Guided Iterative Prompting (Jha et al., 2023) | $\checkmark$ | $x$ | Dialog | - | -

items.fact: The paper 'Med-HALT: Medical Domain Hallucination Test for Large Language Models (Umapathi et al., 2023)' uses the Med-HALT dataset.
entities: Med-HALT: Medical Domain Hallucination Test for Large Language Models
items.evidence_span: Med-HALT: Medical Domain Hallucination Test for Large Language Models (Umapathi et al., 2023) | $X$ | $x$ | Reasoning Hallucination Test (RHT), Memory Hallucination Test (MHT) | Med-HALT | Accuracy, Pointwise score

items.fact: The paper 'Sources of Hallucination by Large Language Models on Inference Tasks (McKenna et al., 2023)' uses the Altered directional inference adatset.
entities: Sources of Hallucination by Large Language Models on Inference Tasks
items.evidence_span: Sources of Hallucination by Large Language Models on Inference Tasks (McKenna et al., 2023) | $\checkmark$ | $x$ | Textual entailment | Altered directional inference adatset | Enatilment probability

items.fact: The paper 'Hallucinations in Large Multilingual Translation Models (Pfeiffer et al., 2023)' uses the FLORES-101, WMT, and TICO datasets.
entities: Hallucinations in Large Multilingual Translation Models
items.evidence_span: Hallucinations in Large Multilingual Translation Models (Pfeiffer et al., 2023) | $\checkmark$ | $\checkmark$ | MT | FLORES-101, WMT, and TICO | spBLEU

items.fact: The paper references SELFCHECKGPT (Manakul et al., 2023)
items.evidence_span: SELFCHECKGPT (Manakul et al., 2023), is a method for zero-resource black-box hallucination detection in generative LLMs.

items.fact: The paper references Mündler et al., 2023
items.evidence_span: Self-contradictory hallucinations in LLMs are explored in (Mündler et al., 2023).

items.fact: The paper references PURR (Chen et al., 2023)
items.evidence_span: PURR (Chen et al., 2023) is a method designed to efficiently edit and correct hallucinations in language models.

items.fact: The paper references Zhang et al., 2023a
items.evidence_span: (Zhang et al., 2023a) proposed a hypothesis that in certain instances when language models attempt to rationalize previously generated hallucinations, they may produce false statements that they can independently identify as inaccurate.

items.fact: The paper references HaluEval (Li et al., 2023b)
items.evidence_span: HaluEval (Li et al., 2023b), is a comprehensive benchmark designed for evaluating hallucination in LLMs.

items.fact: The paper references Ji et al., 2023
items.evidence_span: The survey in (Ji et al., 2023) describes hallucination in natural language generation.

items.fact: (Zhang et al., 2023b) presents a method for mitigating language model hallucination
items.evidence_span: (Zhang et al., 2023b) presents a method for mitigating language model hallucination

items.fact: LLMAUGMENTER (Peng et al., 2023) improves LLMs using external knowledge and automated feedback
items.evidence_span: LLMAUGMENTER (Peng et al., 2023) improves LLMs using external knowledge and automated feedback

items.fact: (Li et al., 2023d) introduces a framework called 'Chain of Knowledge' for grounding LLMs with structured knowledge bases
items.evidence_span: (Li et al., 2023d) introduces a framework called 'Chain of Knowledge'

items.fact: (Elaraby et al., 2023) evaluates and mitigates hallucinations in BLOOM 7B
items.evidence_span: Smaller, open-source LLMs with fewer parameters often experience significant hallucination issues compared to their larger counterparts (Elaraby et al., 2023)
